subtitle: Review recovered obligations and reconciliation pressure without hiding uncertainty behind polished prose.

sections.title: Highest-Impact Recovered Obligations
sections.label: Recovered Obligation
sections.why: Lead with the recovered obligations most likely to constrain design.

sections.title: Validation, Integration, and Data Reconciliation
sections.label: Reconciliation
sections.why: Use a matrix when recovered requirement pressure spans multiple dimensions.

sections.title: Recovered Constraints and Obligations
sections.label: Recovered Constraint
sections.why: Keep recovered constraints dense and directly reviewable.

sections.title: Low-Confidence Areas and Inferred-vs-Observed Distinctions
sections.label: Confidence Gap
sections.why: Keep uncertainty explicit instead of smoothing it away.

sections.title: Findings that Pressure Technical-Design Reconciliation
sections.label: Reconciliation Finding
sections.why: Highlight recovered findings that should push back on design assumptions.

sections.title: Decisions Required for Approval
sections.label: Decision Gate
sections.why: State the calls that let downstream work treat recovered obligations as settled enough.

sections.title: Traceability Map
sections.label: Evidence
sections.why: Tie substantive approval claims to exact canonical evidence.

sections.title: Validator Status
sections.label: Validation
sections.why: Show canonical and approval-view validation status together.

sections.title: Downstream Impact if Approved
sections.label: Impact
sections.why: Clarify what downstream work may now treat as fixed.

glance_cards.label: Reconciliation findings
glance_cards.hint: Findings pushing on downstream design assumptions.

glance_cards.label: Decision gates
glance_cards.hint: Approvals that let downstream artifacts trust the recovered baseline.

glance_cards.label: Evidence claims
glance_cards.hint: Claims backed by exact source quotes.
